what_it_does: It records pixel-level source-verification verdicts for design-system augmentation claims, classifying each as STANDS, CORRECTED, or REFUTED, and identifies which claims may be merged, which recipes must be fixed first, which were bias casualties, and which remain unverified.
observations: 145 verdict rows are reported across 11 faces, collapsing to roughly 130 distinct augmentations after cross-face reconciliation.

The verification law defaults every claim to REFUTED; a claim becomes STANDS only when the cited slide was opened and the rule was seen in the pixels, and CORRECTED when the rule is real but the recipe is wrong.
92 augmentations stand clean, 45 survive only after a corrected recipe, 8 are refuted, and 33 confirming-bias claims were caught and downgraded.
Each face section lists entries with inline corrected recipes, narrowed provenance, or dropped mis-citations.
A cross-face reconciliation section merges duplicate verdicts for the same device across faces and resolves one conflict (density-as-confidence) toward the better-evidenced read.
Several open conflicts and still-unverified items are explicitly held back rather than auto-resolved.
standouts: The verification law requires opening the cited slide and seeing the rule in the pixels before any claim can stand.
92 stand clean, 45 need recipe correction, and 33 confirming-bias 'already-right/covered' claims were downgraded.
The components face contains the most entries and introduces several new molecules and organisms with source-confirmed recipes.
A dedicated confirming-bias casualties section names claims that were filed as resolved but broke under pixel verification.
conventions: Follows a strict evidence hierarchy where pixels outrank docstrings and finding wording.
Uses numbered sections and clear outcome categories: merge set, bias casualties, still-unverified, reconciliation, bottom line.
Repeats explicit warnings not to misread aggregate survival counts as a headline success.
Breaks the convention of trusting synthesized findings by forcing primary-source verification and recording recipe corrections inline.
concerns: Some entries mix verdict, corrected recipe, and editorial commentary in a single bullet, complicating automated extraction.
The figure-gold value conflict is left open inside the merge set rather than resolved.
A few corrected recipes still carry inferred or author-derived framing that may need further review.
The reconciliation states ~130 distinct augmentations without showing the exact deduplication mapping.
notes: This file is the post-verification merge gate: it tells you which design-system augmentations are source-safe to merge, which need a recipe fix, and which must stay out until the actual slide is opened.
questions: Which exact deck files and page numbers correspond to each cited slide reference?
What is the precise definition of a 'face' versus an 'augmentation' in this ledger?
How were the 11 faces selected and what is the complete list?
What is the underlying 'live' design-system state that these augmentations are being compared against?
Which tool or process performed the slide opening and pixel measurement?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the stated purpose is to present a source-checked merge set, and the file does exactly that by listing verdicts, corrected recipes, refutations, bias casualties, and held-back items.
apparent_themes: Distinguishing observed behavior from inferred law.
intention_signals: It is trying to stop falsely-green synthesis from entering the design system by forcing every augmentation through a primary-source pixel check, then surfacing only the source-evidenced subset for merge while flagging bias casualties and unresolved conflicts.
novelty: It includes a formal 'confirming-bias casualties' section that names augmentations filed as 'already correct' or 'extends existing' but broke under pixel verification, making the verification law's corrective value explicit.
contribution: A structured, source-evidenced verdict inventory that separates merge-ready claims from corrected recipes, refuted claims, and still-unverified items, plus a cross-face reconciliation that deduplicates repeated devices across faces.
summary_for_embedding: A verified ledger of design-system augmentation claims across 11 faces, recording pixel-source verdicts (STANDS, CORRECTED, REFUTED), corrected recipes, confirming-bias casualties, still-unverified items, and cross-face reconciliation for merge decisions.
intention_for_embedding: To act as a source-checked merge gate that prevents unverified or overreached design rules from being merged into the live design system.
